title: Amazon Auto Canceling Eva 333 preorders
wikitext:
[]: Amazon Auto Canceling Eva 3.33 preorders.             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Oct 27, 2014 3:07 pm So I got an e-mail from good ole amazon recently which said I had to go make my order active for Eva 3.33 otherwise they were going to automatically cancel the order. I find this hilarious and a potential PR/financial problem for Funi. Imagine how many people simply ignore an e-mail like that and end up not receiving their copy. Not to mention it just makes your company look horrible when it takes you so long to release the DVD that Amazon starts auto canceling orders. Note: Yes I understand they are doing extra work to edit the dub, I just find it sadly hilarious.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 27, 2014 4:01 pm As much as I like Funi and kind of understand the pressure they are under from Khara and Co. to come out with a quality product, it would really, really be nice if they could just get their head back in the game and release something. Anything. Not even the DVD at this point, but a release date or maybe just an approximate release date specifying which financial quarter it will land in. Even if the release date is way off in the middle of next year or something, it would at least give us a set time to look forward to so we wouldn’t be stuck in this limbo. All that being said, I have the Japanese Blu-ray release. And whereas I probably will buy the English release DVD and Blu-ray at some point, I’m not suffering from a lack of Eva Q in my collection at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by TitaniumViper » Mon Oct 27, 2014 4:18 pm Yeah, I just saw that Evangelion 3.33 was removed (currently unavailable) from Amazon. Now this can be taken as a bad thing or possibly it just has been so long that even Amazon is tired of waiting. So I guess the question is did Amazon take action on their own or did Funimation give them the okay to temporarily remove it from the site (possibly due to a longer delay)?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Mon Oct 27, 2014 8:32 pm Us eva fans think we have it bad, but hellsing ultimate took about five years to get released and dubbed by funimation. So hopefully will get Eva 3.33 released before 3.0+1.0 gets released in Japan. You know how hard it will be to avoid spoilers then.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Mon Oct 27, 2014 9:36 pm Did this automatically happen to everyone with a preorder? And did you get your money back?

[]:             Quote Post by SolidEye » Mon Oct 27, 2014 10:27 pm Meanwhile Germany got their blu-rays over a year ago... Well we have proper dubbing unlike the USA anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Oct 27, 2014 10:33 pm Sicarius VI wrote: Did this automatically happen to everyone with a preorder? And did you get your money back?

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Tue Oct 28, 2014 12:17 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: All that being said, I have the Japanese Blu-ray release. And whereas I probably will buy the English release DVD and Blu-ray at some point, I’m not suffering from a lack of Eva Q in my collection at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 28, 2014 2:08 am cyharding wrote: I have a bad feeling that I'm the only regular forum member who still hasn't seen a quality version of the movie yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 28, 2014 3:14 am cyharding wrote: I have a bad feeling that I'm the only regular forum member who still hasn't seen a quality version of the movie yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Oct 28, 2014 10:09 pm Oh I don't believe this was on here, but about a month ago Funimation did a live-stream that shed light on the reason why it is taking so long for 3.33 to release in English. Basically it is all Khara driven, and the way they describe it, I don't think Funi is the problem. This was released Sep. 18, 2014. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fi-IypWVGO0&feature=youtu.be&t=28m26s The 3.33 answer is at the start of their Q&A, which is a little before the middle.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Oct 28, 2014 11:42 pm If Anno and company are micromanaging the release, I wonder how that effects the "dubs are a bastardization!" hardliners' view of the re-translation. If a work's original creators have a significant role in the creation of a dub, it would move, I think, away from the realm of interpretation and becomes more like an extension of 3.33. Less easy for purists to ignore, in other words. We rarely encounter this in anime, so it's an interesting situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Oct 28, 2014 11:44 pm This is only hurting two (Edit: groups of)people, the fans and Funimations Shareholders.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Oct 28, 2014 11:45 pm There's only one each!?

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Oct 28, 2014 11:49 pm Warren Peace wrote: If Anno and company are micromanaging the release, I wonder how that effects the "dubs are a bastardization!" hardliners' view of the re-translation. If a work's original creators have a significant role in the creation of a dub, it would move, I think, away from the realm of interpretation and becomes more like an extension of 3.33. Less easy for purists to ignore, in other words. We rarely encounter this in anime, so it's an interesting situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Oct 28, 2014 11:54 pm It's a nice evidence against the "Anno only cares about money!" meme, at least. If that were true, he could have let Funi release whatever last February and raked in the cash already.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Oct 29, 2014 6:09 am Somehow Anno nearly breaking down post Q and Anno making sure, the English Dub is perfect, thus driving right into Q again contradicts itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Wed Oct 29, 2014 9:05 am In some ways, you can see how this incident proves Anno has deep respect for the English speaking fanbase of Evangelion and wants to ensure we get to experience the most accurate translation of the movie. That being said, it's still terrible for Funi on a financial and reputation standpoint. Maybe I'm in the minority, but I at least like having the option of an English dub available. Especially if you want to watch it with other people, as subs demand a larger degree of attention from the audience then dubs.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Oct 29, 2014 1:19 pm Since I'm not a dub viewer, I'm not very affected by this problem. However, it does seem nice that Anno really wants to make sure that the dub captures what he really means to say. Which, as we've covered before, is certainly important; Q is ambiguous enough that garbled lines would make it nigh impossible to understand.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Wed Oct 29, 2014 2:11 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Since I'm not a dub viewer, I'm not very affected by this problem. However, it does seem nice that Anno really wants to make sure that the dub captures what he really means to say. Which, as we've covered before, is certainly important; Q is ambiguous enough that garbled lines would make it nigh impossible to understand.
